descriptions.description: Albizia adianthifolia is a tree in the family Fabaceae. It is commonly known as the flat-crown. Its range extends from eastern South Africa to Tropical Africa.

This is a large deciduous tree with a spreading, flat crown, growing to a height of 25 m.Pooley, E. (1993). The Complete Field Guide to Trees of Natal, Zululand and Transkei. . A profusion of bright green leaves and heavily scented, fluffy flowers are produced in winter or spring.Purves, M. (2010) The leaves are twice compound with the leaflets being 2–5 x 8 mm in size.Pooley, E. (1993). The Complete Field Guide to Trees of Natal, Zululand and Transkei. . This tree favours sandy soils in warm, high rainfall areas. In South Africa it is found in coastal lowland forests.Pooley, E. (1993). The Complete Field Guide to Trees of Natal, Zululand and Transkei. .

Albizia adianthifolia is cultivated as an ornamental tree. The attractive habit of these trees makes them a popular garden tree, often being retained as a native plant in suburban gardens when other indigenous vegetation is removed.Purves, M. (2010) The trees usually produce abundant seeds which are easily grown in sandy soil.Purves, M. (2010)

Elephants browse the leaves of these trees and blue duiker favour the leaves and seedpods as food.Pooley, E. (1993). The Complete Field Guide to Trees of Natal, Zululand and Transkei. . The larvae of the satyr charaxes butterfly (Charaxes ethalion) feed on the leaves of these trees.Williams, M. (1994). Butterflies of Southern Africa: A Field Guide. .

descriptions.description: Gosliner, Terrence M., David W. Behrens, and Ángel Valdés. 2008. Indo-Pacific Nudibranchs and Sea Slugs. A field guide to the World's most diverse fauna. Sea Challengers Natural History Books and California Academy of Sciences. 426 pp.

Gosliner, Terrence M., David W. Behrens, and Ángel Valdés. 2008. Indo-Pacific Nudibranchs and Sea Slugs. A field guide to the World's most diverse fauna. Sea Challengers Natural History Books and California Academy of Sciences. 426 pp.

Nepenthes × ferrugineomarginata (; from Latin ferrugineus "rust coloured" and marginatus "margined") is a natural hybrid between N. albomarginata and N. reinwardtiana.McPherson, S.R. & A. Robinson 2012. Field Guide to the Pitcher Plants of Borneo. Redfern Natural History Productions, Poole. It has been recorded from the islands of Borneo and Sumatra.McPherson, S.R. & A. Robinson 2012. Field Guide to the Pitcher Plants of Sumatra and Java. Redfern Natural History Productions, Poole. The type specimen was collected by Shigeo Kurata in Kenukat, West Kalimantan, in 1981. Kurata described the hybrid the following year. The name is a nomen invalidum as it was published without a Latin description.Nepenthes × ferrugineomarginata Sh.Kurata. International Plant Names Index (IPNI). Nepenthes × ferrugineomarginata has the longest binomial name of any plant in the genus. Image:Albo-reinwardt.jpg|Lower pitcher with green [[Peristome#In_pitcher_plants|peristome]]
Hypoxis hygrometrica, known as the golden weather-grass, is a small herbaceous plant. It is found in south eastern Australia.Les Robinson – Field Guide to the Native Plants of Sydney, page 233

Gosliner, Terrence M., David W. Behrens, and Ángel Valdés. 2008. Indo-Pacific Nudibranchs and Sea Slugs. A field guide to the World's most diverse fauna. Sea Challengers Natural History Books and California Academy of Sciences. 426 pp.

Gosliner, Terrence M., David W. Behrens, and Ángel Valdés. 2008. Indo-Pacific Nudibranchs and Sea Slugs. A field guide to the World's most diverse fauna. Sea Challengers Natural History Books and California Academy of Sciences. 426 pp.
Hennediella heimii, also known as Heim's pottia, is a moss with 2.5 mm brown or yellow-green tufts.
Hennediella heimii is found in saline environments such as coastal salt marshes, and in recent times on the verges of roads salted to prevent icing.Hennediella heimii BBS Field guide

Gosliner, Terrence M., David W. Behrens, and Ángel Valdés. 2008. Indo-Pacific Nudibranchs and Sea Slugs. A field guide to the World's most diverse fauna. Sea Challengers Natural History Books and California Academy of Sciences. 426 pp.
It is possible that this species is synonymous with Plakobranchus ocellatus.
